title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text: This project was completed as part of the Advanced Operational Modelling module at Dublin City University during my Erasmus exchange. The objective was to act as a management consultant and propose Lean-based improvements for a fictitious Irish hospital emergency department.

title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text: This project was completed as part of the Advanced Operational Modelling module at Dublin City University during my Erasmus exchange. The objective was to act as a management consultant and propose Lean-based improvements for a fictitious Irish hospital emergency department.

title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text:
The Problem






Note



Emergency departments across Ireland face significant pressure from increasing patient volumes, staff shortages, and inefficient processes — leading to long waiting times and patient dissatisfaction.

title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text:
Methodology
Three core Lean tools were applied:

Value Stream Mapping (VSM) — to identify waste and bottlenecks in the patient journey
Kaizen — to propose continuous improvement initiatives
5S — to reorganise physical spaces and improve efficiency

title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text:
Key Findings



Issue
Impact




Redundant triage steps
Increased waiting time


Poor bed management
Patient flow bottlenecks


Disorganised storage
Staff time wasted

title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text:
Recommendations

Implement a fast-track pathway for low-acuity patients
Introduce real-time bed management dashboard
Apply 5S methodology to medication and equipment storage
Run regular Kaizen events with frontline staff

title: Lean Management in Emergency Departments
text:
Key Takeaways
This project strengthened my ability to apply structured problem-solving frameworks to real-world healthcare operations — directly relevant to my career goals in MOA/PMO roles in the health sector.

title: ImpulseCap
text:
ImpulseCap was developed as part of the 26th Challenge Projets d’Entreprendre at IMT Business School (November 2025), a week-long intensive entrepreneurship challenge in collaboration with IMT Starter, Telecom SudParis, and ensiie.
As CEO, I co-led a multidisciplinary team of 5 to design and pitch a startup from scratch in one week.

title: ImpulseCap
text:
ImpulseCap was developed as part of the 26th Challenge Projets d’Entreprendre at IMT Business School (November 2025), a week-long intensive entrepreneurship challenge in collaboration with IMT Starter, Telecom SudParis, and ensiie.
As CEO, I co-led a multidisciplinary team of 5 to design and pitch a startup from scratch in one week.

title: ImpulseCap
text:
The Problem






Note



34% of people with disabilities in France engage in no physical activity (Source: ONAPS/FNORS), often due to a lack of adapted programmes and safety concerns.


Physical inactivity and social isolation are compounded challenges for people with disabilities. Existing fitness solutions do not account for the diversity of disabilities, leaving a significant gap in the market.

title: ImpulseCap
text:
The Solution
ImpulseCap is an adaptive fitness application that generates personalised training programmes based on the user’s disability profile and goals, validated by physiotherapists.

~100 exercises covering motor disabilities at launch
Freemium model with social premium features
Target: certification as a medical device within 24 months

title: ImpulseCap
text:
My Role



Role
Commitment




CEO
80% FTE


Strategy & coordination
Cross-team leadership


Pitch & jury presentation
Lead speaker

title: ImpulseCap
text:
Business Model

Free tier: ad-supported (video ads during recovery time)
Premium: €4.99/month — community messaging, avatar, remote physiotherapist contact

Business Angels: €30,000
Loans: €25,000

title: ImpulseCap
text:
Key Takeaways
This project gave me hands-on experience in entrepreneurship, from market analysis and competitor research to financial modelling and pitching to a jury of entrepreneurs and professors.

title: My Journey
text:

As part of my programme, I completed an Erasmus exchange at DCU. This experience strengthened my ability to work in an international environment and is the context in which this portfolio was created.




2023 – present

2020 – 2023



Before moving into business school, I completed a degree in biology. This background gave me a strong analytical foundation and a natural affinity for the healthcare sector.

title: My Journey
text:
Experience


Junior Project Manager Intern — Customer Experience
GE HealthCare · Buc, France · April 2026 · 22 weeks


Currently contributing to customer experience improvement projects in an international HealthTech environment.

Contribution to customer experience optimisation projects

Process analysis and recommendations



Project Management Customer Experience MedTech International




Procurement Project Management Intern
BioSerenity · 2024


BioSerenity develops connected medical devices for neurological monitoring. My mission focused on coordinating a multi-year PCB component procurement strategy.

Coordinated a 3-year PCB procurement strategy involving 3 internal teams, with CFO-level reporting via SAP Business ByDesign
Managed a Plan A → Plan B pivot following regulatory non-compliance of obsolete components
Tracked and updated stock and order data in SAP Business ByDesign



SAP Business ByDesign Procurement Cross-team coordination Medical devices Risk management
